title: super() should be invoked appropriately
description: "super()" should be invoked appropriatelyAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoIn JavaScript, thesuperkeyword is used to call the constructor and methods of an object’s parent class, and to access its properties.The expressionsuper(...args)is used to call the parent’s constructor. It must be used carefully and correctly to avoid errors.class Dog extends Animal { constructor(name) { super(); this.name = name; super(); // Noncompliant: constructor is called twice. super.doSomething(); } }Follow these instructions when invoking the parent’s constructor:super()cannot be invoked in the constructor of a non-derived class.super()must be invoked in the constructor of a derived class.super()must be invoked before thethisandsuperkeywords can be used.super
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.
